MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Timothy R. Vrastil, Esq., Bish Law, telephone 424-277-7061, appearing for Plaintiff;
James F. Hannawalt, Esq, Deputy ciyt Attorney, CCSF Telephone 415-554-3913, appearing for Defendant CCSF;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to quash trial subpoena of Jeffrey Bramlett is denied. The City's counsel previously agreed to accept service of the subpoena on behalf of Mr. Bramlett, who is a material witness who signed the City's written discovery responses and a declaration in support of the City's motion for summary judgment. Plaintiff Zhongying Ding is not seeking to reopen discovery or to depose Mr. Bramlett. The City can readily resolve Mr. Bramlett's schedule conflict with the current trial date by accepting Plaintiff's offer to stipulate to a brief continuance of the trial until Mr. Bramlett returns from vacation.

Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/EPS)

RULING - The Judge disclosed that he is knowledgeable about the Pan Pacific international exposition, and it is not the pan American exposition. It was the 1915 world's fair that was in the Marina and for which these planters or terrace steps were built. He has seen them many times and admired them and never climbeded on them. The matter was argued. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for dangerous condition of public property and granted as to the second cause of action for failure to warn of a dangerous condition of public property. There is a triable dispute whether the planters constitute a dangerous condition of public property. (See word minutes for details).
